title: Plugin install_from_git: URL-derived plugin name path traversal
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension**: Security and Access (OWASP Input Handling)

**Location**: src/plugins/manager.rs:624-689 (install_from_git), src/plugins/manager.rs:346-394 (add_marketplace_from_git)

**Violation**: The plugin/marketplace name is derived from the URL by splitting on '/' and stripping '.git':
```rust
let name = url.trim_end_matches('/').trim_end_matches(".git").rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or("plugin").to_string();
let dest = plugins_dir.join(&name);
```
A URL like 'https://evil.example.com/foo/..%2F..%2Fsecrets.git' or one containing path separators in the last segment (e.g. 'https://host/a%2Fb') can be crafted so that after unwrap the 'name' contains traversal sequences that join() resolves outside .openclaudia/plugins/. Unlike install_from_marketplace (manager.rs:483-488) which explicitly rejects '..' and '/' in the plugin name, install_from_git and add_marketplace_from_git perform NO such validation. git itself then creates arbitrary directories on disk.

**Mandated Refactor**:
1. Apply the same validation pattern as install_from_marketplace: reject names containing '..', '/', '\\', or leading '.'.
2. After computing `name`, call `Path::new(&name).components().count() == 1 && matches!(components.next(), Some(Component::Normal(_)))`.
3. After clone, canonicalize `dest` and verify `dest.starts_with(plugins_dir.canonicalize()?)`.
4. Sanitize to [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ character class and fail fast on violation.
